text: Working with APIs allows different software systems to communicate with each other. Most APIs use HTTP requests, where your code sends a request and receives a JSON response containing structured data. Many APIs require authentication, typically using an API key or token, to verify your identity. It is important to respect rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a given time period. If you exceed these limits, the API might temporarily block your access. Good error handling is essential when working with APIs. Your code should check for status codes like 404 (not found) or 500 (server error), and handle unexpected responses gracefully. This makes your application more reliable and user-friendly.
